input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable automatically)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Or for .NET 9:
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# See what workloads are available (discovers current versions)
dotnet workload search

# Install MAUI (pulls in Android, Windows, iOS dependencies)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows apps need the Windows SDK and WinUI/XAML tooling. The simplest path:

```powershell
# Install VS 2022 Build Tools with required components
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Then add required workloads via the VS Installer CLI
$vsInstaller = & "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vswhere.exe" `
    -latest -property enginePath
& "$vsInstaller\setup.exe" modify --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
    --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
    --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
    --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Component.Windows11SDK.22621 `
    --passive --norestart
```

> **Or**, if using full VS 2022 Community/Pro, select the **.NET Multi-platform App UI development** workload in the installer — it bundles everything.

## Step 4 — Install Android SDK & Java JDK

The MAUI workload installs Android pieces, but you can verify and manage them:

```powershell
# Check what MAUI provisioned automatically
dotnet workload list

# If you need to manually install Android SDK via the CLI:
# First, find where the Android SDK was placed
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
if (Test-Path $androidSdk) {
    Write-Host "Android SDK found at: $androidSdk"
} else {
    Write-Host "Android SDK not found — installing via maui-check or manually"
}

# List installed Android SDK components
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install/update specific platform versions dynamically
# Discover available platforms:
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install the latest (e.g., android-35):
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-35" "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

### Java JDK (required for Android builds)

```powershell
# MAUI typically bundles Microsoft OpenJDK; verify:
java -version

# If missing, install Microsoft OpenJDK:
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK   # discover available versions
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --accept-source-agreements
```

## Step 5 — Set environment variables

```powershell
# Discover the paths dynamically and persist them
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
$jdkPath = (Get-ChildItem "${env:ProgramFiles}\Microsoft\jdk-*" -Directory |
    Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName

[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", $jdkPath, "User")

# Refresh current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidSdk
$env:JAVA_HOME = $jdkPath
```

## Step 6 — Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows app deployment)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 7 — Create & build a test project

```powershell
# Discover available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0   # adjust TFM to your SDK version

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

### Dynamically discover the right TFMs from the project:

```powershell
# Parse TargetFrameworks from the .csproj
[xml]$proj = Get-Content .\MauiTestApp.csproj
$tfms = $proj.Project.PropertyGroup.TargetFrameworks -split ';'
Write-Host "Available targets: $($tfms -join ', ')"
```

## Step 8 — Android emulator (optional)

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "system-images;android-.*google_apis;x86_64"

# Install an image (pick from output above)
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n MauiEmulator -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --force

# Launch it
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd MauiEmulator
```

## Quick verification checklist

```powershell
# Run all of these — all should succeed
dotnet --version
dotnet workload list | Select-String "maui"
java -version
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --version
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Adjust `net8.0` → `net9.0` throughout if you installed .NET 9
- The Windows TFM version (`10.0.19041.0` vs `10.0.22621.0`) depends on which Windows SDK you installed — the `csproj` file sets the default
- Run steps 1–6 in an **elevated** (Admin) PowerShell where noted
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They want PowerShell commands and dynamic version discovery. Let me provide a thorough, practical guide.
